title: The Oracle
description: Predict the exact output of these tricky algorithms.

questions.question: What is the exact output of this code?
questions.explanation: It prints 0, then 1, then 2. When x becomes 3, the loop stops. Using `print` instead of `println` keeps them on the same line: 012

questions.question: What is the exact output?
questions.explanation: `evens.length` is 4. `4 - 1 = 3`. The item at index 3 is the last element: 8.

questions.question: Predict the return value.
questions.explanation: 5 is passed as `a`, 10 is passed as `b`. `5 > 10` is false, so it falls down to `return b;` which returns 10.

questions.question: What prints to the screen?
questions.explanation: The loop runs twice (i=0, i=1). Each time, it prints the capitalized word "JAVA". Since it uses `print`, it outputs "JAVAJAVA".

questions.question: What does this code do?
questions.explanation: It prints 1, then 2. When i is 3, the `break` destroys the loop entirely before printing. Output is 12.

questions.question: Predict the output:
questions.explanation: `nums[1]` (which was 20) is updated to 99. Then we add `nums[1]` (99) and `nums[2]` (30). 99 + 30 = 129.
